column1: Nigerian Food Price Inflation Analysis — Data Cleaning Summary
Scope: Rice, Beans, Garri, Yam | Nov 2024 – May 2026

column2: Monthly source files use date-specific column names (e.g. 'Average of April-25'), which breaks Append across files.
column3: Renamed columns by role, not date, inside the Transform File function used by 'From Folder' (applies automatically to every file).

column2: Highest/Lowest columns arrived as combined text+number (e.g. 'Oyo (1938.91)'), unusable for sorting/charting.
column3: Split each into two columns using Split Column by Delimiter; converted price fields to Decimal Number.

column2: Year and Month existed as separate fields with no sortable date key; rows loaded out of chronological order.
column3: Added custom Date column: Date.FromText([Month] & " 1, " & Text.From([Year])).

column2: Relied only on NBS's built-in MoM/YoY, no independent verification of calculation logic.
column3: Built MoM_Calc and YoY_Calc from raw price columns; verified they matched NBS originals. Both versions retained, clearly labeled, to demonstrate independent validation.

column2: Nulls present in Avg_Price_LastYear, Highest/Lowest fields for specific items/months (e.g. Local Rice (Broken), Jan 2025).
column3: Traced to genuine NBS source-file gaps and item classification inconsistencies across months. Left as null (no imputation); added Data_Complete flag column.

column2: Recurring negative YoY values for specific items (e.g. Beans Brown, -40% to -50% range across multiple months).
column3: Cross-referenced against CBN Food YoY. Recurring (not isolated) pattern supports real deflation vs. 2023–2024 high base-year prices, not a calculation error.

column2: Table structured wide (one column per region), difficult to filter/chart directly.
column3: Flagged for unpivoting into long format (Item Label, Region, Price, Date) prior to visualization.
column4: NorthCentral, NorthEast, NorthWest, SouthSouth, Southeast, Southwest

column2: Source data recorded multiple (business-day) readings per month; did not match monthly grain of Dim_Date.
column3: Grouped by Year + Month, sorted descending by Date, extracted latest available rate per month as the representative monthly Central_Rate.

column2: PivotTable showed identical repeated value across all dates.
column3: Diagnosed as a broken/inactive relationship to Dim_Date (not a data issue) — confirmed table's Period field was already first-of-month and compatible.

column2: Data type on the Date column was incorrect, breaking relationships to dependent fact tables (most visibly CBN_Exchange_Rate).
column3: Corrected Date column data type in Dim_Date; verified relationship direction (1-to-many, dimension to fact) restored correctly across all connected tables.

column2: No consistent way to compare staple-level trends without grouping item variants (e.g. 3 rice variants).
column3: Added Food_Category column using Text.Contains logic to map item variants to parent staple (Rice, Beans, Garri, Yam).

column2: No lightweight way to trace/inspect specific rows (e.g. for duplicate-checking).
column3: Added Index column (Row_ID) to each fact table for session-level traceability. Note: not a permanent identifier — renumbers on refresh.

column2: Evaluated as a 4th source; investigation revealed structural imbalance and heavy nulls (32%-55% by commodity).
column3: Root-caused to dataset being concentrated in conflict-affected Northeast states (Borno + Yobe = ~68% of rows), consistent with a humanitarian/food-security monitoring dataset rather than a nationally balanced price series. Excluded from core analysis.
